title: Chapter 6 - Paragraphs & Text
description: Check understanding of basic text elements and emphasis.

questions.prompt: Which tag should you use for a paragraph?

explanations.overview:
Use

to mark blocks of text as paragraphs.

byOptionId.p:
Correct — use

for paragraphs of text.
byOptionId.div: Incorrect — <div> is a generic block container but not the semantic paragraph tag.
byOptionId.br:
Incorrect —
inserts a line break, not a paragraph block.
byOptionId.span: Incorrect — <span> is inline and not for paragraph blocks.

explanations.overview: <strong> signals strong importance and is semantically meaningful.

byOptionId.em: Incorrect — <em> indicates emphasis but not the same semantic weight as <strong>.
byOptionId.b: Incorrect — <b> is purely presentational; <strong> adds semantic meaning.
byOptionId.i: Incorrect — <i> is presentational and does not imply importance.

questions.prompt: Which tag creates a horizontal rule (a thematic break)?

explanations.overview: <hr> creates a thematic break line across the content.

byOptionId.hr: Correct — <hr> represents a thematic break in content.
byOptionId.br:
Incorrect —
is a line break, not a thematic rule.
byOptionId.p:
Incorrect —

is for paragraphs, not horizontal rules.
byOptionId.hr2: Incorrect — <hr2> is not a valid HTML element.
